summary.headline: GitHub repository bacoco/seo-geo-growth-agent demonstrates a reusable SEO/GEO skill package.
summary.biggest_blocker: The repository is installable, but the public GitHub page is not itself an owned website with root-level /llms.txt, /for-ai, or owner analytics.
summary.fastest_win: Use this golden audit to verify the report pipeline, then run the full workflow on the real owned domain that will publish the AI-readable files.
summary.decision: Treat this as a real artifact demonstration for the public repository, not as evidence of traffic, ranking, conversions, or AI citations.

findings.title: Repository needs an owned publication surface for AI-readable files
observed: The public repository exposes README.md, INSTALL_FOR_AGENTS.md, SKILL.md, runbooks, templates, scripts, tests, and examples.
A GitHub repository page is not the same as an owned domain root where /llms.txt, /for-ai, /for-ai.json, and /for-ai.txt can be published.
The committed golden audit intentionally keeps traffic, ranking, conversion, and AI-citation metrics unknown.
inferred: The skill package is reusable and installable, but AI-readable publication files still need owner review before being placed on an owned site or docs surface.
recommended: Use the generated AI-layer package as an owner-review draft for a real documentation or website surface.
Publish only the files that match visible owner-approved content.
Keep analytics and citation metrics unknown until owner data exports or measured prompt panels are supplied.

screenshot_status: unavailable: committed golden audit does not include live browser screenshots

summary.verdict: Browser evidence is intentionally omitted from this deterministic committed golden audit.

observed: No owner-hosted ARD ai-catalog discovery signal is included in this GitHub repository page.
recommended: If the publisher wants this skill to be discoverable as an agentic resource, publish an owner-reviewed ai-catalog on an owned domain or documentation surface.
